message:
You've definitely seen it: your dog finishes their business, then dramatically kicks up grass and dirt with their back legs like they're trying to bury it. 🐶 Here's the surprising twist — they're not cleaning up at all. They're doing the exact opposite.

Dogs have scent glands tucked into their paw pads, and that vigorous kicking spreads their personal scent across the ground. The fresh scratch marks act as a visual "I was here" sign, while the pheromones from their paws linger far longer than the smell of poop ever would. It's basically your dog leaving a billboard AND a business card for every other dog in the neighborhood. You'll see wolves, foxes, and coyotes do the very same thing.

So that little post-potty kick isn't bad manners — it's ancient canine communication in action. Pretty impressive for something that mostly just flings dirt onto your shoes. 😄💛

Does your dog do the big dramatic kick? Tell me — full-on grass-flinger, or polite tidy-upper? 🐾
